name: Zigbee 3.0 USB Dongle Plus
ha_notes: Plug and play with ZHA. Supports router mode (extend mesh range). Supports OTA firmware updates. Widely recommended as the go-to coordinator for new HA users.
features: CC2652P chipset with amplifier for better range
OTA firmware updates via Zigbee2MQTT or ZHA
notes: Model: ZBDongle-P | Most popular Zigbee coordinator for Home Assistant. Successor to the older CC2531.
pros: Works with both ZHA and Zigbee2MQTT
cons: USB extension cable recommended to avoid RF interference from PC
May need firmware update out of box

name: Zigbee 3.0 USB Dongle-E
ha_notes: Uses EmberZNet firmware (Silicon Labs stack). Excellent ZHA compatibility. Can also run as Zigbee2MQTT coordinator with proper firmware.
features: Supports multi-PAN for future Thread use
notes: Model: ZBDongle-E | Silicon Labs variant of the popular Sonoff Dongle. Same price as ZBDongle-P but uses EmberZNet stack preferred by ZHA.
pros: EmberZNet stack preferred by ZHA team
cons: Zigbee2MQTT support requires special firmware flashing

name: Home Assistant SkyConnect
ha_notes: Official Home Assistant USB stick. Works out of the box with HA OS. Supports Zigbee (ZHA) and Thread/Matter. Multi-PAN capable for simultaneous Zigbee + Thread. Firmware upgradeable via HA interface.
features: Multi-PAN: run Zigbee and Thread simultaneously
OTA firmware updates via Home Assistant UI
notes: Model: HassOS Connect | Official Nabu Casa / Home Assistant USB coordinator. The recommended starting point for new HA users wanting Zigbee + Thread support.
pros: Easy firmware updates via HA UI
cons: More expensive than basic Sonoff dongles

name: Home Assistant Connect ZBT-1
ha_notes: Successor to SkyConnect under updated branding. Same EFR32MG21B hardware. Works with ZHA for Zigbee and as Thread Border Router. Certified Zigbee coordinator.
notes: Model: ZBT-1 | Updated branding of the Home Assistant Connect USB stick family.
pros: Plug and play with HA OS

name: SLZB-06 Zigbee to Ethernet/USB Coordinator
ha_notes: Connects over Ethernet (TCP) or USB. Network-based mode lets HA server and coordinator be physically separated. Supports Zigbee2MQTT and ZHA via zigbee-herdsman. Web UI for configuration and firmware updates.
features: CC2652P chip with PA for extended range
Can be placed anywhere on your network
notes: Model: SLZB-06 | Popular Ethernet-based Zigbee coordinator. Ideal when the HA server is in a different location from the Zigbee devices.
pros: Ethernet connection—no USB needed on HA server
Can place coordinator anywhere in home for best coverage

name: SLZB-06M Zigbee POE Coordinator
ha_notes: POE-powered version of SLZB-06. Connects over Ethernet TCP. No external power supply needed—single Ethernet cable for both data and power. Ideal for ceiling mounting or hard-to-reach areas.
features: Single cable for power and data
Supports Zigbee2MQTT and ZHA over TCP
notes: Model: SLZB-06M | POE-powered Ethernet Zigbee coordinator. Single cable installation makes it ideal for permanent installations.

name: SLZB-07 Zigbee USB Coordinator
ha_notes: USB variant of the Smlight series. Uses CC2652P7 with 20dBm output power for excellent range. Simple plug-and-play with ZHA or Zigbee2MQTT.
features: Higher RF power than standard CC2652P
notes: Model: SLZB-07 | USB Zigbee coordinator with enhanced 20dBm output power via CC2652P7.
pros: 20dBm output power—better range than most USB sticks
cons: Less community documentation than Sonoff dongles

name: ConBee II Universal Zigbee Gateway
ha_notes: Works with deCONZ software via the Home Assistant deCONZ integration. Also supports ZHA directly. deCONZ provides a visual map of your Zigbee mesh network. Popular with Philips Hue users.
features: Works with deCONZ software for visual mesh map
Supports Philips Hue, IKEA Tradfri, and more
notes: Model: ConBee II | German-engineered Zigbee coordinator. Known for excellent device compatibility, especially Philips Hue. Includes deCONZ GUI for managing the Zigbee network.
pros: Excellent Philips Hue and IKEA Tradfri compatibility
Phoscon web UI for easy device management
Established product with long track record
cons: deCONZ adds complexity vs. simpler ZHA setup
Older Atmel chipset (less powerful than CC2652P)

name: ConBee III USB Gateway
ha_notes: Upgraded successor to ConBee II with modern EFR32MG21 chip. Works with deCONZ and ZHA. USB-C connector. More reliable than its predecessor.
notes: Model: ConBee III | Third-generation ConBee with modern Silicon Labs chipset and USB-C.

name: UZG-01 Universal Zigbee Gateway
ha_notes: Dual mode USB and Ethernet gateway. Open source firmware based on ESP32. Web UI for configuration. Supports Zigbee2MQTT and ZHA via both USB and network. MDNS discovery for easy HA setup.
features: MDNS auto-discovery for easy HA setup
notes: Model: UZG-01 | Open source Zigbee gateway with dual USB and network connectivity from the ZigStar community.
cons: More complex setup than simple USB sticks

name: ZB-GW04 Zigbee Ethernet Coordinator
ha_notes: Ethernet-based Zigbee coordinator by community creator Tube. Based on ESP32 + CC2652P. Connects to HA via TCP serial. Widely trusted in the Zigbee2MQTT community.
notes: Model: ZB-GW04 v2 | Community-designed Ethernet Zigbee gateway. One of the original network-based coordinators in the HA community.
pros: Ethernet-only—no USB on HA server needed
cons: Less polished web UI than Smlight

name: HUSBZB-1 USB Zigbee + Z-Wave Adapter
ha_notes: Dual Zigbee and Z-Wave combo stick. Appears as two separate serial ports in HA. Use ZHA or Zigbee2MQTT for Zigbee; Z-Wave JS for Z-Wave. Great for users wanting both protocols in one dongle.
features: Zigbee and Z-Wave in one USB stick
Works with ZHA and Z-Wave JS simultaneously
notes: Model: HUSBZB-1 | Popular combo stick for users wanting both Zigbee and Z-Wave support from a single USB device. Z-Wave 500 series (older but stable).
pros: Zigbee AND Z-Wave in one USB stick
Works with ZHA, Zigbee2MQTT, and Z-Wave JS

name: zig-a-zig-ah! (zzh!) USB Coordinator
ha_notes: Open source CC2652R-based USB coordinator. One of the first to bring the TI CC2652 chip to the community. Compatible with ZHA and Zigbee2MQTT.
notes: Model: zzh! | Community-designed open source Zigbee coordinator. Pioneered CC2652R adoption in the community.
cons: No PA (power amplifier)—shorter range than CC2652P variants

name: Z-Stick 7 Plus
ha_notes: Works with Z-Wave JS integration in Home Assistant. Supports S2 security and SmartStart (QR code pairing). Use Z-Wave JS UI add-on for advanced management. Plug and play with HA OS.
features: Compatible with Z-Wave JS and OpenZ-Wave
notes: Model: ZWA010 | Most popular Z-Wave 700 series USB stick. The go-to Z-Wave controller for Home Assistant users.
pros: S2 security for encrypted device communication

name: Z-Pi 7 Z-Wave Raspberry Pi HAT
ha_notes: Mounts directly on Raspberry Pi GPIO header. Configure Z-Wave JS to use the HAT's serial port (/dev/ttyAMA0 or /dev/ttyS0). Disable Bluetooth in Raspberry Pi config to free the UART. Best performance when RPi is used as a dedicated HA server.
notes: Model: ZWA025 | Z-Wave 700 HAT for Raspberry Pi. Ideal for dedicated Raspberry Pi Home Assistant servers. Requires UART configuration.

name: ZST10 700 Series Z-Wave USB Stick
ha_notes: Works with Z-Wave JS integration. Good alternative to Aeotec Z-Stick 7. Often sold bundled with Zooz Z-Wave devices. Z-Wave JS UI add-on recommended for full feature access.
notes: Model: ZST10 | Affordable Z-Wave 700 series stick from Zooz. Great value alternative to Aeotec Z-Stick 7.

name: ZST39 800 Series Z-Wave USB Stick
ha_notes: Z-Wave 800 series stick. Works with Z-Wave JS. 800 series offers improved range, better LR (Long Range) support for future devices, and lower power consumption.
notes: Model: ZST39 | Z-Wave 800 series stick from Zooz. Latest generation Z-Wave chipset with Long Range support.
cons: Z-Wave Long Range devices are still uncommon

name: RaZberry 7 Pro Z-Wave GPIO HAT
ha_notes: Raspberry Pi GPIO Z-Wave 700 HAT by Z-Wave.Me. Includes Z-Way software option, but also works with Z-Wave JS. Requires UART config and Bluetooth disable on RPi.
features: Compatible with Z-Way and Z-Wave JS
notes: Model: ZMEERAZBERRY7P | Raspberry Pi Z-Wave HAT from Z-Wave.Me. Competes with Aeotec Z-Pi 7.
pros: Option to use Z-Way or Z-Wave JS

name: UZB-7 Z-Wave USB Controller
ha_notes: Z-Wave 700 series USB stick from Silicon Labs / Z-Wave.Me. Works with Z-Wave JS. Also comes with Z-Way software license. Compact design.
notes: Model: UZB-7 | Z-Wave 700 series USB stick. Also includes Z-Way license for standalone Z-Wave controller functionality.

name: Home Assistant Yellow
ha_notes: All-in-one Home Assistant hub with built-in Zigbee/Thread coordinator (EFR32MG21B). Runs HA OS on Raspberry Pi CM4. Just add a CM4 module. Built-in Zigbee/Thread chip runs ZHA out of the box. Z-Wave requires additional USB stick.
features: M.2 SSD slot for fast storage
Built-in power over Ethernet (POE+) support on some models
USB 3.0 ports for Z-Wave stick addition
notes: Model: HA Yellow | Official all-in-one Home Assistant hub. Requires Raspberry Pi CM4 (sold separately). Built-in Zigbee/Thread coordinator. Best Home Assistant experience without separate coordinator dongle.
pros: All-in-one HA hub—no separate Pi needed
M.2 SSD support for fast, reliable storage

name: Elevation C-8 Smart Home Hub
ha_notes: Hubitat can integrate with Home Assistant via the Hubitat integration. Run as standalone hub or as a complement to HA. Uses its own local automation engine. Zigbee and Z-Wave 700 built in.
notes: Model: C-8 | Standalone smart home hub with built-in Zigbee and Z-Wave. Local processing means automations work without internet. Popular alternative to SmartThings.
pros: Built-in Zigbee AND Z-Wave—no extra dongles
cons: HA integration is limited vs. native HA

name: HomePod mini
ha_notes: Acts as a Thread Border Router and HomeKit hub. Thread devices can be accessed via Matter integration in HA. Requires Apple Home setup first, then Matter device sharing with HA.
notes: Model: HomePod mini | Apple's compact smart speaker is also a Thread Border Router and HomeKit hub. Essential for Apple HomeKit users with Thread devices.
pros: Best Thread Border Router for Apple users
cons: Thread only works well within Apple ecosystem

name: Apple TV 4K (3rd generation)
ha_notes: Thread Border Router and HomeKit hub. Ethernet connection makes it a more reliable Thread BR than WiFi-only devices. Matter devices can be shared to HA via Matter integration.
features: Ethernet port on Wi-Fi + Ethernet model
notes: Model: Apple TV 4K 3rd gen | Streaming device that doubles as a Thread Border Router and HomeKit hub. Ethernet model preferred for reliability.
pros: Ethernet option for reliable Thread BR
cons: Expensive for use as a hub only

name: Nest Hub (2nd gen)
ha_notes: Acts as a Thread Border Router for Google Home. Matter devices added to Google Home can be shared to HA via Matter integration. Also a Google Home hub for remote access to Google smart home devices.
notes: Model: Nest Hub 2nd gen | Google's smart display with built-in Thread Border Router. The most affordable Thread BR for Google Home users.
pros: Affordable Thread Border Router for Google users
Google Home hub for remote access
cons: Thread only meaningful within Google ecosystem

name: Nest Wifi Pro
ha_notes: WiFi 6E mesh router with Thread Border Router built in. Each node acts as a Thread BR, giving excellent Thread coverage throughout home. Matter devices can be shared to HA.
features: Thread Border Router in every node
notes: Model: Nest Wifi Pro | WiFi 6E mesh router with Thread Border Router. Every mesh node is a Thread BR, creating excellent Thread coverage.
pros: Thread BR in every mesh node—great coverage
WiFi 6E upgrade and Thread in one

name: Echo (4th Gen)
chipset: AZ2 Neural Edge (Thread + Zigbee built in)
ha_notes: Amazon Echo 4th Gen has built-in Zigbee hub, Thread Border Router, and Matter controller. Zigbee devices added to Alexa can be accessed in HA via Amazon Alexa integration. Matter devices can be shared to HA directly.
notes: Model: Echo 4th Gen | Amazon's flagship Echo is also a Zigbee hub, Thread Border Router, and Matter controller all in one. Most versatile Echo for smart home use.
cons: HA integration requires Alexa skill setup
Zigbee hub is limited vs. dedicated coordinators

name: nRF52840 USB Dongle
ha_notes: Can be flashed with OpenThread RCP firmware to act as a Thread radio co-processor for Home Assistant's built-in Thread Border Router. Technical setup—for developers and advanced users.
notes: Model: nRF52840 | Developer dongle. Can be flashed as Thread RCP for use with Home Assistant's OTBR (OpenThread Border Router). Not for casual users.
pros: Can act as Thread RCP for HA OTBR

name: Hue Bridge
ha_notes: Native Hue integration in Home Assistant. Just add bridge IP and authenticate. Exposes all Hue lights, switches, sensors, and scenes. Hue only—cannot add non-Hue Zigbee devices.
features: Control up to 50 Philips Hue lights
Works with Alexa, Google, HomeKit, SmartThings
notes: Model: BSB002 | The Philips Hue hub. Required for all Hue Zigbee products. Excellent ecosystem integration. Limited to Hue devices only (closed Zigbee network).

name: SmartThings Hub v3
ha_notes: Use SmartThings integration in HA. Devices added to SmartThings appear in HA. Cloud-dependent integration. Local control limited. Better to use dedicated HA coordinators if HA-first.
features: Works with Alexa and Google Home
notes: Model: GP-U999SJVLGDA | Samsung SmartThings Hub with Zigbee and Z-Wave. Good standalone hub but HA integration is cloud-based.
pros: Works with Alexa and Google Home
cons: Less control than native HA with dedicated dongles

name: Echo Plus (2nd Gen)
ha_notes: Built-in Zigbee hub for Alexa. Add Zigbee devices directly to Alexa without a separate hub. HA integration via Amazon Echo integration (requires Alexa skill). Note: Echo Plus is discontinued; Echo 4th gen is the current model with Zigbee+Thread.
features: Direct Zigbee device pairing via Alexa app
No separate hub required for Zigbee
notes: Model: Echo Plus 2nd Gen | Discontinued Amazon Echo with built-in Zigbee hub. Superseded by Echo 4th gen which also adds Thread and Matter. Still found used/refurbished.

name: DIRIGERA Smart Home Hub
ha_notes: IKEA's hub for TRADFRI and SYMFONISK devices. Has native Home Assistant integration. Successor to the TRADFRI Gateway. Supports Matter for interoperability. Use IKEA integration in HA.
notes: Model: DIRIGERA | IKEA's modern hub replacing the old TRADFRI gateway. Required for IKEA smart home devices. Matter support allows cross-platform use.
pros: Works with all IKEA smart devices
cons: Requires IKEA Home app for setup

name: Smart Home Hub (SmartThings compatible)
ha_notes: Aeotec-branded SmartThings hub (Samsung licensed to Aeotec). Runs SmartThings OS. Works with SmartThings integration in HA. Cloud-dependent integration.
features: Works with Alexa and Google Home
notes: Model: Smart Home Hub | Aeotec's SmartThings-compatible hub. Effectively the SmartThings Hub v3 under a different brand after Samsung licensed the platform.

name: Zigbee Ethernet Coordinator v3
ha_notes: Community-made Ethernet Zigbee coordinator. Newer v3 hardware with improved design. TCP serial connection to HA. Works with Zigbee2MQTT and ZHA. Popular in the HA community forums.
notes: Model: ZB-COORD-V3 | Improved Ethernet Zigbee gateway from TubesZB community creator.

name: Zigbee USB Dongle (CC2652P)
ha_notes: Budget CC2652P coordinator from Waveshare. Works with ZHA and Zigbee2MQTT. Plug and play. Good alternative when Sonoff dongles are out of stock.
notes: Model: Zigbee-USB-Dongle-B | Budget Zigbee coordinator from Waveshare. Solid CC2652P performance at a low price.

name: SLZB-06p7 Multi-Protocol POE Coordinator
ha_notes: Advanced POE coordinator supporting Zigbee, Thread, and Matter over Ethernet. CC2652P7 with 20dBm output. Firmware can be flashed for Thread/Matter RCP mode. Multi-protocol future-proof design.
notes: Model: SLZB-06p7 | Next-generation Smlight coordinator supporting Zigbee + Thread + Matter over POE Ethernet. Highest output power in the line.

name: ZST10 700 Series Z-Wave Plus S2 USB Stick
ha_notes: Affordable Z-Wave 700 stick. Works seamlessly with Z-Wave JS in HA. S2 security and SmartStart supported. Often bundled with Zooz Z-Wave devices for a discounted combo price.
features: Works with Z-Wave JS and OpenZ-Wave
notes: Model: ZST10 700 | Zooz Z-Wave 700 USB stick. Best value Z-Wave controller. Often bundled with Zooz sensor/switch packs.
